# Weight Data — Instrument Comparison

<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

Supporting data for the French Horn Effort Rubric. All claims cite sources in [citations.md](../citations.md).

---

## French Horn Weights by Model

Data from the Ricardo Matosinhos horn weight survey [1], the most granular publicly available dataset. Weights are horn-only (no case, no mouthpiece unless noted).

### Single Horns (1.3-1.7 kg / 2.9-3.7 lbs)

| Model | Type | kg | lbs |
|---|---|---|---|
| E. Schmid high F | Single HF | 1.3 | 2.9 |
| Finke Single Bb | Single Bb | 1.5 | 3.3 |
| Yamaha YHR 314II | Single F | 1.7 | 3.7 |
| E. Schmid Single Bb w/ A-valve | Single Bb+A | 1.7 | 3.7 |
| Finke Single Bb + stopped valve | Single Bb+A | 1.7 | 3.7 |

### Children's / Vienna Horns (1.4-2.0 kg / 3.1-4.4 lbs)

| Model | Type | kg | lbs |
|---|---|---|---|
| Finke Compact | Children Bb | 1.4 | 3.1 |
| M. Jiracek Vienna 109D | Vienna | 1.5 | 3.3 |
| Ulman Vienna | Vienna | 1.5 | 3.3 |
| Gara Single Bb (Children wrap) | Children Bb | 1.7 | 3.7 |
| Thoman HR 101 (Children wrap) | Children F | 2.0 | 4.4 |

| Children/Vienna | 1.4-2.0 | 3.1-4.4 |
| Double | 2.0-2.7 | 4.4-5.9 |
| Triple | 2.2-3.3 | 4.9-7.3 |

---

## Holton Farkas H279 — Weight Estimate

No manufacturer-published weight exists for the H279 [4]. Estimated weight based on:

- **Holton 177** (fixed-bell sibling, same Kruspe wrap, same bore): 2.5 kg / 5.5 lbs [1]
- **Nickel silver body** adds density vs. yellow brass. Nickel silver (copper-nickel-zinc alloy) is ~8.7 g/cm³ vs. yellow brass at ~8.5 g/cm³ — roughly 2-3% denser.
- **Detachable screw bell** mechanism adds slight weight over fixed bell.
- **Estimate: 2.5-2.7 kg / 5.5-5.9 lbs** — upper end of double horn range.

This places the H279 among the heavier doubles in the Matosinhos survey, comparable to the Alexander 103, Hoyer 7802, and Durk D3 (all 2.7 kg / 5.9 lbs) [1].

---

## Bb Clarinet Weights

Data from Musical Instrument Hub [2]. Note: source quality is moderate (see citation notes).

| Model | Weight (g) | Weight (lbs) |
|---|---|---|
| Buffet R13 | 714-770 | 1.57-1.70 |
| Selmer Privilege | 772 | 1.70 |
| Yamaha YCL-857-II | 700-760 | 1.54-1.68 |
| Plastic student clarinets | ~680 | ~1.50 |
| Wooden clarinets (general) | ~770 | ~1.70 |

**Typical Bb clarinet range: 680-770 g / 1.5-1.7 lbs** [2]

An A clarinet (pitched in A) is approximately 3.5-5% heavier than an equivalent Bb clarinet [2].

---

## Comparative Brass Weights

From BrassHero [3]. Note: these figures are generally higher than specialist surveys and may include case weight or use different measurement methods.

| Instrument | Weight Range (lbs) |
|---|---|
| French Horn | 6-8 |
| Trumpet (Bb) | 2-2.8 |
| Trombone | 3-6 |
| Euphonium | 9-11 |
| Tuba | 20-30 |

---

## Weight Ratio: Clarinet to French Horn

Using the most reliable data (Matosinhos survey [1] for horn, Musical Instrument Hub [2] for clarinet):

| Comparison | Clarinet (kg) | Horn (kg) | Ratio |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lightest clarinet vs. lightest double | 0.68 | 2.0 | 2.9× |
| Typical clarinet vs. typical double | 0.73 | 2.4 | 3.3× |
| Typical clarinet vs. H279 (est.) | 0.73 | 2.6 | 3.6× |
| Heaviest clarinet vs. heaviest double | 0.77 | 2.7 | 3.5× |
| Typical clarinet vs. heaviest triple | 0.73 | 3.3 | 4.5× |

**Summary: A double horn is approximately 3-4× the weight of a Bb clarinet. The Holton H279 specifically is estimated at 3.4-3.7× clarinet weight** (computed as 2.5-2.7 kg horn ÷ 0.73 kg typical clarinet = 3.42-3.70).

---

## Caveats

- The Matosinhos survey is crowd-sourced, not independently lab-measured [1].
- Clarinet weights from [2] are unconfirmed against manufacturer datasheets.
- BrassHero [3] French horn weights (6-8 lbs) appear inflated relative to the Matosinhos model-by-model data. The Matosinhos data is preferred as the more granular and credible source.
- Weight alone does not determine fatigue — holding position, arm extension, and support points are critical factors (see [ergonomics-data.md](ergonomics-data.md)).
